annotation.moveRationale: This offbeat, dubious gambit attacks the g4 pawn immediately, but allows White to take the pawn with 2. * Puts opponent off their comfort zone.
f5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Grob Opening.
The Coca-Cola gambit is mostly a meme opening. It has never been played by masters, but it is recognised by the chess websites Lichess and Chess.
Alternatively known as the Double Grob. The Symmetrical Variation is a rare response to Grob's Opening (1.
b3 prepares the fianchetto, aiming to place the bishop on a long diagonal in the Grob Opening.
dxc4 impacts the center, shaping the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
c4 gains space on the queenside in the Grob Opening.
g5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Grob Opening.
Black plays c6, declines the gambit in order to avoid tricky situations and defends d4. The position for black looks like Caro-Kann Defense.
gxh5 exchanges material, altering the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
h5 advances a pawn, influencing the structure in the Grob Opening.
Qxa8 exchanges material, altering the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
Bxa8 exchanges material, altering the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
Nd7 develops a piece to an active square in the Grob Opening.
Bxb7 exchanges material, altering the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
d4 impacts the center, shaping the pawn structure in the Grob Opening.
c4 gains space on the queenside in the Grob Opening.

c4, the Fritz Gambit is another move where white gambits a pawn.

This move is interesting not because it is a commonly-played move, nor because it gives an advantage to either player (though Black should still be far better), but because White has not one, but two moves that lead to an early checkmate for Black. transposes to the From Gambit of the Bird’s Opening, but this matters little because black can easily mate.

__NOTOC__

Grob's Attack is generally considered to be one of the worst starting moves, and is possibly the single worst starting move, for White. However, if White knows enough theory and Black is caught by surprise, this opening can be playable even at relatively high levels.
